onboarding.title: Rémanence
onboarding.tagline: WHAT REMAINS WHEN THE MUSIC STOPS
onboarding.p1: You're going to experience things that are hard to put into words.
onboarding.p2: Sets that move through you. Raw, almost physical energy. Moments where time stretches — and sometimes, the exhaustion that follows.
onboarding.p3: Rémanence is here to keep the trace. In your language, your perception, and your words.
onboarding.nameLabel: WHAT NAME SHOULD WE WRITE IN YOUR TRAVEL JOURNAL?

games.title: Games 🎮
games.subtitle: Explore the festival differently
games.treasureSub: 30 secrets to reveal in the festival
games.introspectionSub: Gentle questions to celebrate our existence

Rouge.label: Red
Rouge.challenge: Find & photograph 21 red moments around you

Orange.label: Orange
Orange.challenge: 21 shades of orange captured before the timer stops

Jaune.label: Yellow
Jaune.challenge: Capture 21 yellow flashes: lights, outfits, decor

Vert.label: Green
Vert.challenge: 21 green tones in nature or the stage design

Bleu.label: Blue
Bleu.challenge: 21 blues — sky, outfits, light shows — in 1 hour

Indigo.label: Indigo
Indigo.challenge: Hunt down 21 indigo & violet shades in the camp

Cercle.label: Circle
Cercle.challenge: Photograph 21 perfect circles in the festival

Triangle.label: Triangle
Triangle.challenge: Capture 21 triangles hidden in the structures

Losange.label: Diamond
Losange.challenge: Capture 21 diamonds in outfits and installations

Arc.label: Arc
Arc.challenge: Find 21 curves and arcs in the environment

Corps peint.label: Body paint
Corps peint.challenge: Capture 21 festivalgoers with visible body paint or body art

Ailes & plumes.label: Wings & feathers
Ailes & plumes.challenge: Photograph 21 wearers of wings, feathers or elaborate headpieces

Tatouage.label: Tattoo
Tatouage.challenge: Find 21 remarkable tattoos — large area, unique style, unexpected placement

Déguisement.label: Costume
Déguisement.challenge: 21 complete costumes — animal, creature, character or universe

Accessoire fou.label: Crazy accessory
Accessoire fou.challenge: Photograph 21 impossible accessories — headpieces, glasses, jewelry, objects

UV & néon.label: UV & neon
UV & néon.challenge: 21 outfits or elements that glow or would glow under UV night lights

Masque & fard.label: Mask & makeup
Masque & fard.challenge: Capture 21 masked or painted faces with artistic intention

Tribu & clan.label: Tribe & clan
Tribu & clan.challenge: 21 coordinated groups — same color, style, symbol or shared universe

introspection.title: 💭 Introspection
introspection.subtitle: Gentle questions to celebrate our existence
questions: What has surprised you most in this festival so far?
What sound will still haunt you after you've gone home?
If you had to keep just one image from this festival, what would it be?
What do you want to remember in ten years?
What would you be afraid of forgetting first?
Which moment did you wish you could freeze forever?
If you could live one hour of this festival on loop, which would it be?
What are you taking with you when you leave?
Which emotion don't you want to forget before you go home?
What is the music telling you tonight?
What single word best describes how you feel right now?
What made you smile for no reason today?
What is your body telling you it loves?
What state do you arrive in? What state do you leave in?
What unexpected emotion came over you today?
What made you dance even when you didn't want to?
What do you allow yourself here that you don't allow yourself elsewhere?
Which part of you came here without being invited?
Which version of yourself did you bring here?
What is this festival teaching you about yourself?
Which boundary have you pushed, even slightly?
What do you keep for yourself alone among everything you're experiencing?
What did you accept today that you refused yesterday?
What are you leaving behind by coming here?
Who are you thinking about right now?
Who did you meet that left a mark, even briefly?
Which face stays in your memory without you knowing why?
What gesture or look from a stranger touched you?
If you could give this festival to someone, who would it be?
What does the crowd teach you about solitude?
What did you never imagine you'd experience here?
What does the night allow that the day doesn't?
If this moment had a smell, what would it be?
What silence spoke to you the most today?
Which sound went through you rather than passing by?
Which sense is most alive in you right now?
What seemed magical while actually being ordinary?
What texture or light did you notice that you never see in the city?
Which artist made you feel something unexpected?
What art or performance touched you enough to make you stop?
If you had to write a song about today, what would its title be?
If this festival wrote you a letter, what would be in it?
If this festival were a color, which would it be?
If this festival were a person, what would they be like?
What do you miss here? What fills you up?
What does this place awaken in you?
If this festival were a dream, what time would you wake up?
What would you do differently if you started from the beginning?
What has the music healed in you?
What is the most beautiful thing you've seen today?

treasure.title: 🗺️ Treasure Hunt
treasure.subtitle: Explore the festival, reveal its secrets
treasure.hintMeetings: If you want to meet people
treasure.allDoneMsg:
You traversed the festival with your eyes wide open.
Ozora holds you in its memories.
secrets: An artist creating or painting a work live
Someone playing an acoustic instrument outside an official stage
A juggler, acrobat or fire artist in the middle of a performance
A real exchange with someone from another continent
Someone freely distributing food, water or an accessory
A creator or craftsperson selling a fully handmade work
A group of people dancing in a circle, hands joined or arms raised
Someone sharing a story around a fire or under the stars
Someone lying in the grass, eyes closed, smiling at the world
Someone in deep meditation, still, in the heart of the festival
A fully fluorescent outfit glowing under UV night lights
Someone dressed as a fantastical, tribal or utterly mysterious creature
A hairstyle or hat that is absolutely unique, never seen elsewhere
A tattoo or body painting covering more than half the body
A color worn as clothing that you'd never have dared yourself
Someone wearing an adornment made entirely of natural materials
A light installation or sculpture you hadn't yet noticed
A totem or giant decoration dominating the campground
A pattern or texture that really looks like a fractal
A musical instrument left alone, without its owner in sight
The exact spot where two stages create an unexpected harmony
A handcrafted luminous, bioluminescent or strange object you can't name
The precise instant when you feel fully here — nowhere else in the world
A brazier, torch or campfire lit in the night
A laser or beam of light cutting through the darkness like a blade
The starry sky visible from the dancefloor — no buildings, no screens
A natural mirror: puddle, lake or surface perfectly reflecting the sky
A wild animal: bird, giant insect, fox, butterfly, stray cat…
A sunrise or sunset seen from the dancefloor or campground
A healing or silence space in the heart of the festival (massage, yoga, circle)

theories.title: 🧠 Absurd Theories

variants.label: Defense
variants.desc: Defend this theory for 30 seconds without being interrupted. Others listen, then judge.

variants.label: Group vote
variants.desc: Each player says whether this theory could be true. The majority wins.

variants.label: Collective extension
variants.desc: Each person adds an element to the theory, in order. The final version belongs to everyone.

theories: DJs don't really mix: they communicate with the crowd through telepathy.
Psytrance music is an alien language encoded on human frequencies.
Bass is actually an energy recharging system for humans.
Every drop briefly opens a portal to another dimension.
DJs know exactly when someone in the crowd needs a drop.
BPM controls the collective heartbeat of the festival.
DJs are secretly testing new musical styles from the future.
Festival music directly influences local weather.
Dancers create the music with their movements — DJs just follow.
Each DJ is chosen by a secret council of musical shamans.
Psytrance festivals are organized to welcome extraterrestrials.
Some festivalgoers are actually undercover aliens studying human happiness.
The large decorations serve to communicate with a distant civilization.
Lasers are used to send coded messages into space.
Extraterrestrials use festivals to identify the most awakened humans.
Psytrance is the official music of the known universe.
Aliens only come for the sunrise sets.
Some DJs come from other planets and go home after each festival.
Fractal decorations are maps of distant galaxies.
Extraterrestrials think festivals are the normal form of human society.
The festival is a simulation created to test the limits of human joy.
Time passes differently inside a festival.
The festival only exists because participants collectively believe in it.
The festival is a secret social experiment conducted for decades.
Each festival creates a small temporary civilization with its own laws.
Festivals are dress rehearsals for a new society.
People find each other at festivals because they knew each other in past lives.
The festival completely disappears from reality when the last person leaves.
The festival is an invisible city that reappears at the same spot each year.
The festival grounds hold the vibrational memory of previous years.
Festivals only happen when the stars are perfectly aligned.
Dancers collectively create an energy field visible from space.
The sunrise during a set recharges the balance of the universe.
Festivals are convergence points of earth energy.
Fractals represent the actual structure of the cosmos — and DJs know it.
Festivalgoers unconsciously influence the energetic balance of the Earth.
Psytrance rhythms follow a cosmic rhythm engraved in matter since the Big Bang.
Sunsets are more beautiful during festivals because the Earth is happy.
Festivals are modern cosmic ceremonies whose origin no one remembers anymore.
The universe observes festivals to understand human humor.
Tents move slightly at night when no one is watching.
Camping neighbors quietly change each night without anyone noticing.
Tents are portals to a gentler version of the dream world.
Air mattresses have a very basic but real consciousness.
Headlamps attract nocturnal creatures only children can see.
Objects lost at a festival join a parallel dimension full of pairs of sunglasses.
Festival campgrounds are the oldest nomadic villages of modern humanity.
Mosquitoes are secret agents collecting behavioral data.
Tents have acoustic memory of all nighttime conversations.
The best camping spots only appear for people who don't need them.
Glitter is a secret currency used by a festival elite.
Mirror sunglasses grant observation superpowers limited to 48 hours.
People become better dancers after midnight because gravity slightly decreases.
Festivals were invented by comfortable shoe manufacturers to test their products.
The festival trees are the real organizers — humans just execute.
Festivals exist solely to teach humans to smile at strangers.
Artists secretly observe the crowd to learn how to dance themselves.
Each festival creates a completely novel dance style that disappears at the end.
People systematically become more honest at festivals — no one knows why.
Festivals are the primitive form of a future society no one has a plan for yet.
